title: U.K. plans first G7 digital sovereign bond by early 2027
dek: The U.K. Treasury will issue its first digital sovereign bond, called DIGIT, by early 2027 on HSBC's Orion platform, making Britain the first G7 nation to place government debt on distributed-ledger infrastructure. Chancellor Rachel Reeves announced the timeline in her Mansion House speech. The Bank of England plans to make DIGIT eligible as collateral in its market operations.
author: Crypto Cronkite
key_fact: The U.K. will become the first G7 nation to issue government debt on a distributed-ledger infrastructure by early 2027.
bottom_line: Watch for the Treasury's disclosure of DIGIT's issuance terms and settlement asset, and track whether other G7 nations announce similar tokenized debt programs. The story's premise invalidates if DIGIT fails to achieve collateral eligibility at the BoE, or if the Treasury delays issuance beyond early 2027.
body: Britain will issue its first digital sovereign bond by early 2027, positioning itself as the first G7 nation to place government debt on distributed-ledger infrastructure. Chancellor Rachel Reeves announced the timeline in her annual Mansion House speech to industry leaders, according to CoinDesk's reporting.

The bond, called the Digital Gilt Instrument (DIGIT), will be a sterling-denominated government security issued on HSBC's Orion platform. It will operate inside the Bank of England and Financial Conduct Authority's Digital Securities Sandbox, per the announcement. HSBC was appointed to run the platform in February, having issued over $3.5 billion in digital bonds through its Orion blockchain.

The initial sale will sit outside the government's conventional gilt-financing program. The Treasury has not disclosed the bond's size, maturity, coupon, investor eligibility, or settlement asset. The government plans further issuance after the initial sale.

The Treasury announced the pilot in 2024 to test whether blockchain infrastructure could reduce settlement times, reconciliation work, and operating costs. Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey said the central bank will work to make DIGIT eligible as collateral in its market operations. Eligibility as collateral could support tokenized repo and allow banks to use the bond in central bank funding transactions, according to CoinDesk.

The move represents a scaled move from pilot to live issuance by a developed-economy treasury, backed by explicit central bank collateral eligibility. What remains unknown: the specific terms of the initial issuance, whether remaining technical or operational risks exist from the 2024 pilot phase, and how tokenized government debt will interface with conventional gilt markets.
